document.title: Essential Principles of Safety and Performance of Medical Devices

sourceSummary.oneLine: Essential Principles checklist for Portable X-ray System (HnX-P1) documenting applicability and conformity evidence.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports medical device regulatory registration by mapping applicable essential principles to risk management, safety, EMC, software, usability, clinical evaluation, labeling, and other supporting documents.
keyPoints: The checklist control number is A-EPC-HNX.
The product owner is H&abyz Co., Ltd.
The product is Portable X-ray System (HnX-P1).
Multiple general requirements are marked applicable and supported by risk management, safety testing, EMC testing, software validation, and clinical evaluation files.
Radiation protection and ionising radiation requirements are marked applicable for the device.
Several biological, sterile, implantable, therapeutic radiology, and lay-person use requirements are marked not applicable.

evidence.claim: General safety and essential performance evidence cites Safety Test report DK-163496-M1-UL for IEC 60601-1:2005/AMD2:2020.
evidence.evidenceText: Safety Test report: DK-163496-M1-UL (IEC 60601-1:2005/AMD2:2020)

evidence.evidenceText: EMC test report No.: KES-EM243955-R2 (EN IEC 60601-1-2:2014+A1:2020)

evidence.evidenceText: (Wireless) EMC Test report No.: KES-EM243956-R1 (EMC Test) Test report No.: KES-RF240917-R1

evidence.claim: Risk management evidence includes plan A-RMP-HNX Rev.4 dated 260226 and report A-RMR-HNX Rev.5 dated 260303.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk Management file Plan: A-RMP-HNX(Rev.4), 260226 Report: A-RMR-HNX(Rev.5), 260303

evidence.claim: Software evidence includes Software Development plan A-SDP-HNX Rev.5 dated 260226 and Software Validation report A-SVR-HNX Rev.4 dated 260226.
evidence.evidenceText: Software Development plan: A-SDP-HNX(Rev.5), 260226 Software Validation report: A-SVR-HNX(Rev.4), 260226

evidence.evidenceText: Clinical Evaluation file Plan: A-CEP-HNX(Rev.4), 260303 Report: A-CER-HNX(Rev.4), 260303

evidence.claim: The device is stated not to be related with medicinal products or substances.
evidence.evidenceText: This device is not related with medicinal products or any substances
evidence.pageTarget: Medicinal Product Incorporation in Medical Devices

evidence.claim: The device is stated not to be intended for sterilisation.
evidence.evidenceText: This device is not intended for sterilisation.

evidence.claim: Radiation protection requirements are applicable and supported by risk management, safety testing, EMC testing, wireless EMC testing, and detective quantum efficiency testing.
evidence.evidenceText: 12. Protection against radiation ... A Risk Management General safety and essential performance test EMC test (Wireless) EMC Test Determination of the detective quantum efficiency test

evidence.claim: The device is intended to emit ionising radiation for diagnostic radiology, with requirements 12.5.1 and 12.5.2 marked applicable.
evidence.evidenceText: 12.5 Ionising radiation ... 12.5.1 Medical devices intended to emit ionising radiation ... A ... 12.5.2 Medical devices emitting ionising radiation intended for diagnostic radiology ... A

evidence.claim: The device is not intended for therapeutic radiology.
evidence.evidenceText: 12.5.3 ... N/A N/A It is not intended for therapeutic radiology.

evidence.claim: The device depends on an internal power supply.
evidence.evidenceText: 13.4 ... N/A N/A The device depends on an internal power supply.

evidence.claim: The device is indicated as a digital imaging solution for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy.
evidence.evidenceText: The device is indicated as a digital imaging solution designed for providing the general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy.

evidence.claim: The device is not designed for lay persons and is for radiologist use only.
evidence.evidenceText: The device is not designed for lay persons, not applicable. Radiologist use only.

evidence.claim: Clinical investigations on human subjects are stated to be carried out in accordance with the spirit of the Helsinki Declaration, but the device is not for clinical investigation.
evidence.evidenceText: Clinical investigations on human subjects shall be carried out in accordance with the spirit of the Helsinki Declaration. ... The device is not for clinical investigation, not applicable with this matter.

reviewFlags.reason: Authority or jurisdiction is not stated in the source text; authoritySignal metadata says FDA but was not used as a document authority.
No overall checklist revision or version is stated; only control number and referenced document revisions are present.
Referenced dates such as 260226 and 260303 are preserved only in evidence text because the source does not define their date format.
Some source text appears extracted from a table with repeated or malformed entries, including incomplete user manual identifiers such as A-IFU-.
